message:
bd sync: close beads_rust-ku1s, beads_rust-lsht

- beads_rust-ku1s: Sync safety E2E tests complete (73+ tests)
- beads_rust-lsht: Cross-platform normalization implemented

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -242,12 +242,12 @@
 {"id":"beads_rust-kj5","title":"config Command Implementation","description":"## Overview
Implement the `br config` command for viewing and modifying br configuration. Supports the layered configuration system (CLI > env > project > user > SQLite > defaults).

## CLI Interface
```
br config [SUBCOMMAND] [OPTIONS]

Subcommands:
  get <key>                 Get a config value
  set <key> <value>         Set a config value
  unset <key>               Remove a config value
  list                      List all config values
  edit                      Open config in editor
  path                      Show config file paths
  init                      Create default config file

Options:
  --global                  Use user-level config (~/.config/br/config.yaml)
  --project                 Use project-level config (.beads/config.yaml)
  --db                      Use SQLite-stored config
  --show-source             Show where each value comes from
  --json                    Output as JSON
```

## Technical Requirements

### Configuration Layering
Priority order (highest to lowest):
1. CLI flags (--prefix, etc.)
2. Environment variables (BR_PREFIX, etc.)
3. Project config (.beads/config.yaml)
4. User config (~/.config/br/config.yaml)
5. SQLite config table
6. Hard-coded defaults

```rust
pub struct ConfigResolver {
    cli_args: HashMap<String, String>,
    env_prefix: &str,
    project_config: Option<Config>,
    user_config: Option<Config>,
    db_config: HashMap<String, String>,
    defaults: HashMap<String, String>,
}

impl ConfigResolver {
    pub fn get(&self, key: &str) -> Option<ConfigValue> {
        // Check each layer in order
        if let Some(v) = self.cli_args.get(key) {
            return Some(ConfigValue { value: v.clone(), source: ConfigSource::Cli });
        }
        if let Some(v) = std::env::var(format!("BR_{}", key.to_uppercase())).ok() {
            return Some(ConfigValue { value: v, source: ConfigSource::Env });
        }
        // ... continue through layers
    }
    
    pub fn get_all_with_sources(&self) -> Vec<(String, ConfigValue)> {
        // Return all config keys with their effective values and sources
    }
}
```

### Config File Format (YAML)
```yaml
# .beads/config.yaml
prefix: bd                    # Issue ID prefix
default_priority: 2           # Default priority for new issues
default_type: task            # Default issue type
auto_flush: true              # Auto-export after changes
sync_on_init: true            # Import JSONL on init if newer
editor: ${EDITOR:-vim}        # Editor for edit commands

# Output preferences
color: auto                   # auto, always, never
format: human                 # human, json, robot

# Paths
database: .beads/bd.db        # SQLite database path
jsonl_dir: .beads             # JSONL export directory
```

### YAML-Only vs SQLite Keys
From the documentation:
- **YAML-only**: editor, color, format, paths (never stored in SQLite)
- **SQLite-capable**: prefix, default_priority, default_type, auto_flush

### Environment Variables
```
BR_PREFIX          - Issue ID prefix
BR_DEFAULT_PRIORITY - Default priority
BR_COLOR           - Color output (auto/always/never)
BR_FORMAT          - Output format
BR_DATABASE        - Database path
```

## Output Formats

### config list (human)
```
Configuration (merged from all sources):

Key               Value          Source
───────────────────────────────────────────
prefix            bd             project (.beads/config.yaml)
default_priority  2              default
default_type      task           default
auto_flush        true           user (~/.config/br/config.yaml)
color             auto           env (BR_COLOR)
editor            vim            default
```

### config list --json
```json
{
  "values": {
    "prefix": { "value": "bd", "source": "project" },
    "default_priority": { "value": "2", "source": "default" }
  },
  "paths": {
    "project": ".beads/config.yaml",
    "user": "/home/user/.config/br/config.yaml"
  }
}
```

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] config get <key> returns effective value
- [ ] config set <key> <value> writes to appropriate config
- [ ] config unset <key> removes config value
- [ ] config list shows all values with sources
- [ ] --global flag targets user config
- [ ] --project flag targets project config
- [ ] --db flag targets SQLite config
- [ ] config path shows all config file locations
- [ ] config edit opens editor
- [ ] Environment variable override works
- [ ] Layering priority is correct

## Unit Tests
- Default values returned when no config
- Project config overrides default
- User config falls back when no project config
- Env vars override file config
- CLI args override everything
- YAML-only keys never read from SQLite
- set/get round-trip works
- unset removes value

## Dependencies
- SQLite Storage Layer (for db config)
- Model Types (Config struct)

## Rationale
Layered configuration allows users to set project-specific preferences while maintaining personal defaults. The config command makes this system transparent and debuggable.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"feature","estimated_minutes":0,"created_at":"2026-01-16T07:18:34.67238432Z","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","updated_at":"2026-01-16T07:49:40.702920628Z","closed_at":"2026-01-16T07:49:40.702920628Z","close_reason":"Duplicate of beads_rust-tqs (config command aligned to updated config system)"}

 {"id":"beads_rust-kr5i","title":"E2E tests: label command","description":"# E2E Tests for `label` Command

## Commands to Test
- `br label add <issue_id> <label>` - Add label to issue
- `br label remove <issue_id> <label>` - Remove label from issue
- `br label list` - List all labels in workspace
- `br label --json` - JSON output mode

## Test Cases
### Success Paths
1. Add single label, verify via show
2. Add multiple labels to same issue
3. Remove label, verify removed
4. List all labels across issues
5. Add same label to multiple issues

### Error Cases
6. Add label to non-existent issue → error
7. Remove non-existent label → error or no-op
8. Invalid label format (if any validation)

### Edge Cases
9. Label with special characters
10. Very long label name
11. Case sensitivity (bug vs BUG)
12. Label on closed issue

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Test file: tests/e2e_labels.rs
- [ ] 12+ test functions
- [ ] Verify label persistence in JSONL export","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"GreenSparrow","owner":"jeff141421@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-01-17T09:25:51.989260181-05:00","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","updated_at":"2026-01-17T09:44:10.369726951-05:00","closed_at":"2026-01-17T09:44:10.369726951-05:00","close_reason":"Implemented 16 E2E tests for label command in tests/e2e_labels.rs - all tests passed","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"beads_rust-kr5i","depends_on_id":"beads_rust-oxmd","type":"parent_child","created_at":"2026-01-17T09:27:48.736022459-05:00","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone"}]}

 {"id":"beads_rust-lhk","title":"Unit tests: storage invariants (real SQLite)","description":"Add unit tests for SQLite storage invariants: schema shape, id prefix/short hash format, dedup/content-hash behavior, label/dep CRUD, ready/blocked queries, and sort/filter correctness.","acceptance_criteria":"1) Tests exercise SqliteStorage APIs against a real temp DB (no mocks).
2) Invariants validated: schema columns/indexes, deterministic IDs, dedup rules, ready/blocked computations, label/dep edges.
3) Tests are deterministic and pass under cargo test.","notes":"Added storage invariant tests in tests/storage_invariants.rs:
- schema tables/columns present
- label CRUD roundtrip
- dependency CRUD + blocked cache contents
- ready filters exclude blocked/deferred + label AND
- list filters (title/prio/include_closed + limit)
- content_hash lookup via upsert_issue_for_import
Also added #![allow(dead_code)] in tests/common helpers to avoid warnings when compiling integration tests.
Ran: cargo test --test storage_invariants (pass).","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","estimated_minutes":0,"created_at":"2026-01-16T16:17:52.471247185Z","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","updated_at":"2026-01-16T16:42:24.265879571Z","closed_at":"2026-01-16T16:42:23.21507082Z"}

 {"id":"beads_rust-m34c","title":"Conformance: Sync Commands (flush, import, roundtrip, status)","description":"# Conformance: Sync Commands

## Background
The sync command is critical for the SQLite+JSONL hybrid architecture. It must export to JSONL identically and import consistently. This is where most data integrity concerns arise.

## Current Coverage
- `sync_flush_only` - Basic export
- `sync_import` - Basic import
- `sync_roundtrip` - Export then import

## New Tests to Add

### sync --flush-only (8 tests)
1. `conformance_sync_flush_empty_db` - Export with no issues
2. `conformance_sync_flush_single_issue` - Export one issue
3. `conformance_sync_flush_many_issues` - Export 100 issues
4. `conformance_sync_flush_with_dependencies` - Dependencies in JSONL
5. `conformance_sync_flush_with_labels` - Labels in JSONL
6. `conformance_sync_flush_with_comments` - Comments in JSONL
7. `conformance_sync_flush_jsonl_line_format` - Verify JSONL line structure
8. `conformance_sync_flush_preserves_order` - Issue order in JSONL

### sync --import-only (8 tests)
1. `conformance_sync_import_empty_jsonl` - Import empty file
2. `conformance_sync_import_single_issue` - Import one issue
3. `conformance_sync_import_many_issues` - Import 100 issues
4. `conformance_sync_import_with_dependencies` - Import deps
5. `conformance_sync_import_with_labels` - Import labels
6. `conformance_sync_import_with_comments` - Import comments
7. `conformance_sync_import_updates_existing` - Import updates in-place
8. `conformance_sync_import_malformed_error` - Error on bad JSONL

### sync roundtrip (6 tests)
1. `conformance_sync_roundtrip_preserves_all_fields` - No data loss
2. `conformance_sync_roundtrip_unicode` - Unicode survives roundtrip
3. `conformance_sync_roundtrip_special_chars` - Special characters preserved
4. `conformance_sync_roundtrip_timestamps` - Timestamps preserved
5. `conformance_sync_roundtrip_dependencies` - Deps survive roundtrip
6. `conformance_sync_roundtrip_100_issues` - Larger dataset roundtrip

### sync --status (4 tests)
1. `conformance_sync_status_clean` - DB and JSONL in sync
2. `conformance_sync_status_dirty` - DB has changes not in JSONL
3. `conformance_sync_status_jsonl_newer` - JSONL has changes not in DB
4. `conformance_sync_status_json_output` - JSON format for status

### sync edge cases (6 tests)
1. `conformance_sync_concurrent_access` - Two processes syncing
2. `conformance_sync_interrupted_recovery` - Recovery from partial sync
3. `conformance_sync_merge_conflict_markers` - Reject files with merge markers
4. `conformance_sync_large_description` - Large text fields (100KB)
5. `conformance_sync_tombstones` - Deleted issues in sync
6. `conformance_sync_incremental` - Only dirty issues exported

## Total: 32 new sync conformance tests

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] All 32 tests implemented and passing
- [ ] JSONL format exactly matches bd output
- [ ] Roundtrip is lossless
- [ ] Error cases handled identically

## Notes
- Sync is the most sensitive operation for data integrity
- Test with both fresh and existing databases
- Verify dirty tracking works correctly","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"CrystalBay","owner":"jeff141421@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-01-17T10:09:45.564505165-05:00","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","updated_at":"2026-01-17T11:04:15.48081266-05:00","closed_at":"2026-01-17T11:04:15.48081266-05:00","close_reason":"Implemented 18 new sync conformance tests (21 total including 3 existing): flush tests (empty_db, single_issue, many_issues, with_dependencies, with_labels, jsonl_line_format, with_comments), import tests (empty_jsonl, single_issue, many_issues, updates_existing), roundtrip tests (preserves_all_fields, unicode, special_chars), status tests (clean, json_output - br-only since bd doesn't support sync --status), edge case tests (large_description, tombstones).  {"id":"beads_rust-mgpi","title":"E2E tests: orphans command","description":"# E2E Tests for `orphans` Command

## Commands to Test
- `br orphans` - List issues referenced but not found
- `br orphans --json` - JSON output

## Test Cases
### Success Paths
1. No orphans → empty list
2. Create dependency to non-existent issue → orphan detected
3. Delete issue with dependents → orphan created
4. Multiple orphan references

### Error Cases
5. Check orphans before init → error

### Edge Cases
6. Self-reference (should not be orphan)
7. Deleted issue in JSONL but not DB
8. Orphan in external_ref field
9. Large number of orphan references

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Test file: tests/e2e_orphans.rs
- [ ] 9+ test functions
- [ ] Verify orphan detection after sync operations","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"BoldHarbor","owner":"jeff141421@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-01-17T09:26:18.402779837-05:00","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","updated_at":"2026-01-17T13:29:06.125977776-05:00","closed_at":"2026-01-17T13:29:06.125977776-05:00","close_reason":"E2E tests in tests/e2e_orphans.rs (13 tests for git-commit based orphans, json/robot/details/empty cases)","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"beads_rust-mgpi","depends_on_id":"beads_rust-oxmd","type":"parent_child","created_at":"2026-01-17T09:27:48.964869123-05:00","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone"}]}
